metadata.title: Name It: Generate 10 Names (5 Descriptive + 5 Creative)
metadata.playlist: Name It & Brand It — Your Strategic Identity
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 6 brief — Brand Strategy + Naming

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 3 of 7. Part 2A of the assignment is ten names: five descriptive and five creative. The rule is generate wide first, judge later. Every good name starts in a long list of names that are not good yet. This episode teaches the split, the rationale format, and the one trap that kills most naming sessions before they start. Let's go.

props.segment: Name It: Generate 10 Names (5 Descriptive + 5 Creative)
props.command: 10 names: 5 descriptive + 5 creative. Rationale + category + impression score (1–10) for each.
props.runningText: generate wide first — judge later; ten variations of one idea is not ten names…
output: Each: 2–3 sentence rationale + category + impression score 1–10

beats.narration_text: Descriptive names clearly communicate what the tool does or who you are. Someone who has never heard of the tool reads the name and has a reasonable idea of its function. Examples of the form: NewsFilter, AnalystBrief, SourceTrace, DigestEngine, SignalRank. For a personal brand in data science: DataAuditKid, MLPipelineAnna, SourceFirstAlex. The descriptive category trades memorability for instant comprehension — a first-time user can make a plausible guess at what they are about to use. For each of the five descriptive names, write two to three sentences: what the name communicates directly, which word in the name carries the most functional signal, and a first-impression score from one to ten — where one is forgettable and ten is immediately clear and distinct. The score is your honest assessment, not a grade you want. A four is useful data. A ten on all five means you did not generate wide enough.

artifactLines: Descriptive = someone reads it and guesses function correctly.
Example forms: [Function][Noun], [Action][Tool], [Domain][Role].
Per name: 2–3 sentence rationale + which word carries the functional signal.
Impression score 1–10: honest — a 4 is useful data. All 10s means the pool was too narrow.
The name should reflect the Part 1 brand, not fight it.
props.sparkLine: First-time reader makes a correct guess. That is the bar.

beats.narration_text: Creative or abstract names are distinctive and memorable — they do not describe the function directly, but they stick. Examples from the technology space: Slack, Notion, Figma, Vercel. None of those names tells you what the product does. They all sound like something. For a news digest AI tool: Serac, Meridian, Gravel, Fable. For a personal brand: Kira Labs, Tao.Dev, Vander. Creative names trade instant comprehension for longevity — a descriptive name can become dated as the tool evolves, while an abstract name grows with the product. For each of the five creative names, write two to three sentences: what feeling or association the name evokes, whether it is pronounceable and searchable, and the impression score. A name that is hard to pronounce loses points in word-of-mouth. A name with no clear phoneme structure is a search problem. The creative category rewards names that are distinctive enough to be Googled in isolation and come back as the intended result. One more test: say it out loud five times. If it sounds wrong on the third repetition, it is not the name.

props.artifactHeading: Distinctive + memorable — trades comprehension for longevity
artifactLines: Creative/abstract = doesn't describe function; sticks in memory.
Per name: evoked feeling/association · pronounceable? · searchable? · impression score.
Pronounce test: say it 5 times — if it sounds wrong on the 3rd, it is not the name.
Search test: distinctive enough to Google in isolation and return as the intended result.
Category: trades instant comprehension for longevity as the tool evolves.
props.sparkLine: Sounds like something. Sticks. Googles. Holds up over time.

beats.act: the trap — ten variations of one idea
beats.narration_text: The trap. Ten variations of one idea is not ten names — it is one idea ten times. If your descriptive list is: NewsBot, NewsAI, NewsFilter, NewsEngine, NewsDigest, you have one idea with five synonyms for the second word. The assignment asks for range. A strong list of ten names will contain names that feel different from each other — some short, some longer; some functional, some abstract; some that sound technical, some that sound human. One quick test: if you gave all ten names to a stranger with no context and asked them to group them, would they put all ten in the same group? If yes, the list needs to be wider. Use AI to generate, then apply human taste to filter. The prompt in the Your Turn section generates a list wide enough to pick from. Your job after AI generates is to read them as a human and ask: which of these would I be proud to introduce at a job interview? Which could I put on a business card? Those are your ten. Not the ten AI likes — the ten you would actually use.

props.artifactTitle: The trap — ten variations of one idea
artifactLines: NewsBot, NewsAI, NewsFilter, NewsEngine, NewsDigest = one idea × 5. Not 5 names.
Strong list: short + long, functional + abstract, technical + human — different in feel.
Stranger test: if someone groups all 10 in one category → list too narrow.
AI generates the pool. Human applies taste: which 10 would I use in an interview or on a card?
Your 10, not AI's 10 — you're choosing, not accepting.
props.sparkLine: Range first. Taste second. Ten ideas, not one idea ten times.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Name Generation. Ten names: five descriptive — clearly communicate function, someone can guess what the tool does from the name — and five creative or abstract — distinctive and memorable, trades instant comprehension for longevity. For each name: a two-to-three sentence rationale, the category, and an impression score from one to ten. The trap is ten variations of one idea. Use AI to generate a wide pool, then apply your human taste to select the ten you would actually use. The names should reflect the brand foundation from Episodes 1 and 2 — a name that fights the positioning is the wrong name. Episode 4 takes the top five to Justia for trademark validation.

props.artifactTitle: Name It: Generate 10 Names (5 Descriptive + 5 Creative)
artifactLines: 5 descriptive (function legible from name) + 5 creative/abstract (distinctive, memorable).
Per name: 2–3 sentence rationale + category + impression score 1–10.
Trap: 10 variations of 1 idea. Fix: stranger test — can all 10 be grouped in one bucket?
AI generates the pool. Human taste selects the 10.
Names must reflect the Part 1 brand. Name that fights positioning = wrong name.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Here is the prompt that generates the wide name pool. Paste it into Claude and fill in the brackets. The prompt reads: I need to generate naming candidates for [personal brand / a startup tool]. The tool's function is: [one sentence from tool_summary]. The primary user is: [paste primary_user]. The brand pillars are: [list Promise, Personality, Differentiation from Episode 2]. The brand personality traits are: [the 3–5 adjectives from the Personality pillar]. Generate 20 naming candidates: ten descriptive names that clearly communicate what the tool does, and ten creative or abstract names that are distinctive and memorable. For each name, write: the category (descriptive or creative), two to three sentences of rationale, whether it is pronounceable in English, and an impression score from one to ten where ten is immediately clear and distinctive and one is forgettable or confusing. Do not generate ten variations of the same root word — vary the structure, length, origin, and feel. After you have the list, read each name out loud. Apply your own taste to pick five descriptive and five creative names from the full list. Write a two-to-three sentence rationale for each of your ten selections. Those ten are Part 2A.

props.segment: Name It: Generate 10 Names (5 Descriptive + 5 Creative)
props.command: Tool function: [1 sentence]. Primary user: [paste]. Brand personality: [3–5 traits]. Generate 20 candidates (10 desc + 10 creative): category · 2–3 sentence rationale · pronounceable? · score 1–10. Vary structure/length/feel — no 10 variations of one root.
props.runningText: read the list aloud — apply your own taste to pick your 10; those are Part 2A, not AI's 10…

beats.narration_text: Name It: Generate 10 Names (5 Descriptive + 5 Creative). Liam, in for Bear.
